--- RESULT ---
## Where workspace/symbol is implemented (end-to-end) ### LSP request handler (rust-analyzer crate) Workspace symbols are served by `handle_workspace_symbol`: - **/testbed/crates/rust-analyzer/src/handlers/request.rs** (around lines 643+) ```rust pub(crate) fn handle_workspace_symbol( snap: GlobalStateSnapshot, params: WorkspaceSymbolParams, ) -> anyhow::Result<Option<lsp_types::WorkspaceSymbolResponse>> { let config = snap.config.workspace_symbol(None); let (all_symbols, libs) = decide_search_scope_and_kind(&params, &config); let query = { let query: String = params.query.chars().filter(|&c| c != '#' && c != '*').collect(); let mut q = Query::new(query); if !all_symbols { q.only_types(); } if libs { q.libs(); } if config.search_exclude_imports { q.exclude_imports(); } q }; let mut res = exec_query(&snap, query, config.search_limit)?; ... Ok(Some(lsp_types::WorkspaceSymbolResponse::Nested(res))) } ``` This calls: - `snap.analysis.symbol_search(query, limit)` (from the `ide` crate), and converts each `NavigationTarget` to `lsp_types::WorkspaceSymbol`. ### IDE API (ide crate) - **/testbed/crates/ide/src/lib.rs** ```rust pub fn symbol_search(&self, query: Query, limit: usize) -> Cancellable<Vec<NavigationTarget>> { self.with_db(|db| { symbol_index::world_symbols(db, query) .into_iter() .filter_map(|s| s.try_to_nav(db)) .take(limit) .map(UpmappingResult::call_site) .collect::<Vec<_>>() }) } ``` ### Symbol index & query engine (ide-db crate) - **/testbed/crates/ide-db/src/symbol_index.rs** Key parts: - `world_symbols(db, query)` builds/uses `SymbolIndex` instances created from `hir::symbols::SymbolCollector`. - `Query::search_maps` filters symbols for “only types”, “exclude imports”, hides `__*` unless queried, etc. Notable filter that can impact enum variants depending on config: ```rust let non_type_for_type_only_query = self.only_types && !matches!( symbol.def, hir::ModuleDef::Adt(..) | hir::ModuleDef::TypeAlias(..) | hir::ModuleDef::BuiltinType(..) | hir::ModuleDef::TraitAlias(..) | hir::ModuleDef::Trait(..) ); if non_type_for_type_only_query { continue; } ``` If the server is in “OnlyTypes” mode, **variants are excluded** because `hir::ModuleDef::Variant(..)` is not in the allowed set. ### Actual symbol collection (hir crate) - **/testbed/crates/hir/src/symbols.rs** This is where items get into the index at all. The collector explicitly handles enum variants: ```rust match def { ... ModuleDefId::EnumVariantId(id) => { this.push_decl(id, name, false, None); } } ``` So: **variants are collectable**, but they may still be filtered out later (see “OnlyTypes” above), or not present in the def-map scope depending on how names are exposed (see hypotheses). --- ## Why enum variants might be excluded (likely causes) ### 1) Workspace symbol is running in “OnlyTypes” mode (most likely) If `all_symbols` is false, rust-analyzer does: ```rust if !all_symbols { q.only_types(); } ``` And `Query::only_types()` excludes everything except ADTs / traits / type aliases / builtin types / trait aliases. That excludes `hir::ModuleDef::Variant(..)`. **When does that happen?** - If the client/server config chooses `WorkspaceSymbolSearchKind::OnlyTypes` (default in some setups), and - the query does not contain `#` (marker for “all symbols”). Hypothesis for fix: - ensure `workspace.symbol.search.kind` defaults to `AllSymbols` (config-level), or - adjust `only_types` filter semantics if variants should be considered “types” for workspace symbol UX (probably not desired globally), or - in the LSP layer, treat some queries as “all symbols” even without `#` (e.g., if query matches variant patterns)—but that’s a behavior change. ### 2) Variants may not be present in the module scope being indexed (possible) Even though `hir/src/symbols.rs` has a `ModuleDefId::EnumVariantId` arm, variants must actually appear in the `DefMap`’s `ItemScope` iteration for the module. If variants are not inserted into the scope entries that the collector iterates, they won’t be collected for workspace search. (This depends on how the collector walks `scope` later in the file—beyond the snippet we inspected—and how `hir_def` exposes variants in scopes.) Hypothesis for fix: - confirm the collector’s traversal includes enum variant definitions from enums (some collectors only see “top-level” scope defs, not sub-items). - if missing, extend collection to, when encountering an `EnumId`, also iterate its variants and push them. ### 3) A UI/LSP mapping issue is unlikely `NavigationTarget` and LSP conversion already support variants: - **/testbed/crates/ide/src/navigation_target.rs** maps `hir::ModuleDef::Variant(it)` into nav targets and uses `SymbolKind::Variant`. - **/testbed/crates/rust-analyzer/src/handlers/request.rs** maps `nav.kind` via `to_proto::symbol_kind`. So if variants are present in `symbol_search` results, they should serialize fine. --- ## Existing tests relevant to workspace symbols ### Tests exercising the symbol index (includes enums; likely not variants) - **/testbed/crates/ide-db/src/symbol_index.rs** has tests: - `test_symbol_index_collection` (golden file) - `test_doc_alias` - `test_exclude_imports` The golden file exists and is referenced here: - **/testbed/crates/ide-db/src/test_data/test_symbol_index_collection.txt** (this file exists and shows collected symbols; quick grepping didn’t immediately show enum variants inside it, suggesting they may not be collected or are not in the excerpt we searched). ### Tests exercising `Analysis::symbol_search` output formatting - **/testbed/crates/ide/src/navigation_target.rs** includes: - `test_nav_for_symbol` - `test_world_symbols_are_case_sensitive` - `test_ensure_hidden_symbols_are_not_returned` These test `analysis.symbol_search(...)` directly, but none appear to assert presence of enum variants specifically. ### LSP-level workspace/symbol tests I found config/capability references and slow-tests harness mentioning workspace symbols, but no clear dedicated LSP test asserting variant presence in `workspace/symbol` responses in the snippets reviewed. The main logic is in `handle_workspace_symbol` (path above). --- ## Concrete “exact locations” to change / inspect for a fix 1) **Filter that drops variants in OnlyTypes mode** - **/testbed/crates/ide-db/src/symbol_index.rs** (`Query::search_maps`, the `non_type_for_type_only_query` block) 2) **Logic that decides OnlyTypes vs AllSymbols** - **/testbed/crates/rust-analyzer/src/handlers/request.rs** (`decide_search_scope_and_kind` inside `handle_workspace_symbol`) - **/testbed/crates/rust-analyzer/src/config.rs** (defines `WorkspaceSymbolConfig`; not opened here, but it’s the source of defaults) 3) **Collector inclusion of variants** - **/testbed/crates/hir/src/symbols.rs** (collector’s `ModuleDefId::EnumVariantId` handling is present; verify later parts of `collect_from_module` actually encounter variant defs from enums) --- ## Summary hypotheses for why variants are missing + fix directions 1) **Most probable:** the request is in “types only” mode (`q.only_types()`), so variants are filtered out intentionally. Fix direction: set/configure `WorkspaceSymbolSearchKind::AllSymbols`, or change the default, or alter how `#`/config maps to `only_types`. 2) **Also possible:** variants aren’t being collected into the symbol index because the collector doesn’t traverse enum internals from an enum definition (despite having an arm for `EnumVariantId`). Fix direction: update `SymbolCollector` in **/testbed/crates/hir/src/symbols.rs** to explicitly collect enum variants when processing an enum item. If you tell me whether variants are missing only when using “types-only” search (no `#` / OnlyTypes config), I can narrow this to the most likely single root cause.
</output>

content.content: Command running in background with ID: bd7301d. Output is being written to: /tmp/claude/tasks/bd7301d.output

content.text: Fixed workspace symbol search to include enum variants. ## Root cause Enum variants weren’t being added to the symbol index at all. The symbol collector (`crates/hir/src/symbols.rs`) only indexed items present in the module scope; enum variants are nested inside enums and weren’t collected. ## Fix - `crates/hir/src/symbols.rs`: when collecting an enum (`AdtId::EnumId`), also collect all of its variants, with `container_name` set to the enum name and `is_assoc = true` so they behave like associated items in search/filtering. ## Tests - Updated/added a regression assertion by changing `crates/ide/src/navigation_target.rs`’s `test_nav_for_symbol` to search for `Bar` and assert it’s returned. - Updated the existing expect-test golden output: - `crates/ide-db/src/test_data/test_symbol_index_collection.txt` regenerated (because the index now contains enum variants). All tests pass (`cargo test`).

All tests pass (`cargo test`).
